author: Robert Lucier
title: Fred Benson Town Beach
summary: Fred Benson Town Beach is commonly referred to as Town Beach and is in the middle of Crescent Beach. Town Beach is popularly known for the pavilion. Inside the pavilion are public bathrooms, lockers, changing rooms, showers, and Rebecca's food stand to buy food, drinks, snacks, ice cream, and more! Additionally, there is a rental stand where one can rent umbrellas and beach chairs.
content:
Fred Benson Town Beach is commonly referred to as Town Beach and is in the middle of Crescent Beach. Town Beach is popularly known for the pavilion. Inside the pavilion are public bathrooms, lockers, changing rooms, showers, and Rebecca's food stand to buy food, drinks, snacks, ice cream, and more! Additionally, there is a rental stand where one can rent umbrellas and beach chairs.

Town Beach is staffed with lifeguards making it a safe place for all to swim.

There is free parking in a beach parking lot in front of Town Beach.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Mansion Beach
summary: Mansion Beach was once the view from Edward F. Searles Mansion, the most notable home on Block Island at the time. The mansion tragically burned down in the 1960s yet the ruins of the once magnificent mansion remain in the parking area of Mansion Beach.
content:
Mansion Beach was once the view from Edward F. Searles Mansion, the most notable home on Block Island at the time. The mansion tragically burned down in the 1960s yet the ruins of the once magnificent mansion remain in the parking area of Mansion Beach. 

Mansion Beach is a unique beach on Block Island with dark sand, and the waves are always perfect for boogie boarding, skimboarding, and body surfing. Mansion Beach is an excellent beach for families and surf lovers. 

From the Block Island Ferry terminal, travel to Corn Neck Road. Continue down Corn Neck Road around 2 miles until you reach Mansion Road on the right. Mansion Road is a long, narrow, bumpy, dirt road. Please drive slowly down Mansion Road and be mindful of bikers, and NO mopeds are allowed down the dirt road. During the summer, the parking lot fills up quickly.

meta.title: Mansion Beach - Block Island
meta.description: Name after a mansion that once stood near this spectacular beach.

address: Mansion Beach New Shoreham New Shoreham

author: Robert Lucier
title: Scotch Beach
summary: A quarter-mile north of Town Beach is the sandy Scotch Beach. Scotch Beach is often a hot spot for young adults, tourists, and island workers. Scotch beach has room for everyone, so bring some chairs, towels, a cooler, and an umbrella to have yourself the perfect beach day!
content:
A quarter-mile north of Town Beach is the sandy Scotch Beach. Scotch Beach is often a hot spot for young adults, tourists, and island workers. Scotch beach has room for everyone, so bring some chairs, towels, a cooler, and an umbrella to have yourself the perfect beach day! 

There is a small parking lot for Scotch Beach off Corn Neck Road. If the parking lot is full, most cars park alongside the road.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Eli's Restaurant
summary: Eli's is a fun, much loved, small bistro located on Chapel Street in the heart of Block Island's historic Old Harbor. Eli's opened its doors in 1994 and was named as a tribute to the original owner's beloved Black Lab, Eli.
content:
Eli's is a fun, much loved, small bistro located on Chapel Street in the heart of Block Island's historic Old Harbor. Eli's opened its doors in 1994 and was named as a tribute to the original owner's beloved Black Lab, Eli.

Constantly striving to offer fresh, innovative locally-sourced cuisine, craft cocktails, and its famous welcoming fun atmosphere.


 

Eli's Menu

 


Open 7 nights a week - 5 pm to 10 pm.



Erika Monat, our Executive Chef, started her career as a culinary student at Southeastern Voc. Tech in Easton, MA from 2009 until her graduation in 2013. She then moved on to New England Culinary Institute in Vermont for two years. During her first year at NECI, she started her internship at Eli's and has been here for the last five seasons.

She has worked her way up to Executive Chef; and she enjoys working in our small kitchen. Eli's local atmosphere, and the freedom to showcase her creativity on the menu, help her to be in tune with her love of being a Chef. Erika lives on Block Island 9 months of the year with her dog Creme, and in Massachusetts in the winter.

author: Robert Lucier
title: Block Island Beach House
summary: Overlooking Crescent Beach, the iconic Surf Hotel at Block Island Beach House offers the only beachfront hotel rooms on the island (some even offer private oceanside balconies). With bold, cheerful, vintage-inspired styling, The Surf hotel rooms are intimate in size and close to the action.
content:
Overlooking Crescent Beach, the iconic Surf Hotel at Block Island Beach House offers the only beachfront hotel rooms on the island (some even offer private oceanside balconies). With bold, cheerful, vintage-inspired styling, The Surf hotel rooms are intimate in size and close to the action.

A brand-new addition to our property, The Gables buildings located directly across the street offer light and airy suites and guest rooms outfitted in soothing neutrals and beach-inspired textures. Well suited to families or a group of friends, these rooms offer more space, direct access to our brand new in-ground pool, and a tranquil ambience.



 

Book A Room

 



Our easygoing, open lounge welcomes guests and neighbors to grab a drink, linger over a snack or meal, or browse beach essentials, vintage-inspired goods, and locally-made gifts. Our brand new, in-ground pool, which sits adjacent to The Gables, is only the second hotel pool on the island!



Located right in the center of town, our charming front porch, sandy backyard, and expansive ocean views make Block Island Beach House the perfect home base for exploring the island or just enjoying the simplicity of summer.



The Surf Restaurant
At Block Island Beach House we believe that a major part of making an experience memorable is the eating and drinking — and we take that seriously. At The Surf Restaurant, we have everything you want, right on site.

Open to all, from hotel guests to locals to day-trippers, you can sip frosé on the beach or gaze at our sunset views while dining on the porch. Visit us to find three distinct, but integrated spots imbine, nosh, and kick back and soak it all in.



 

The Surf Menu

author: Austin Crawford
title: Atlantic Inn
content:
Perched atop the highest point in Old Harbor overlooking town and the ocean beyond, the Atlantic Inn sits in a peaceful setting on manicured grounds just outside town, the beaches, and all Block Island has to offer. The six-acre prop­er­ty offers qui­et sur­round­ings of rolling lawns and care­ful­ly tend­ed gar­dens with stun­ning water views and fresh ocean breezes. The Atlantic Inn’s newly remodeled rooms and suites, each individually furnished with a blend of modern and vintage furniture, await your arrival. In addition to comfort and design-forward appointments, our guest rooms offer air conditioning, luxury linens and bath products, private en-suite bath, and Wi-Fi.

Kick back and relax on our veranda and lawn! It is always a magical time at the Atlantic Inn. Relax on our lawn and take in the sites and sounds of the island. Watch boats come and go in Old Harbor and watch epic sunsets while relaxing high atop the island's premier spot for dining and cocktails.





Our Rooms & Cottage Suites

Newly remodeled, the Atlantic Inn’s 23 air-conditioned rooms and suites blend Victorian charm with modern luxury. Each room, uniquely decorated with a mix of modern and vintage furniture, offers elegant fabrics and luxury pieces. Located on the second and third floors, all accommodations provide sweeping views of the island, ocean, or surrounding fields and are accessible by stairs only. Each room features carefully designed interiors, luxury linens, and bath products in a private en suite bath.

 


Book Now

 



Exquisite Dining

Our talented Executive Chef Francis Cavanagh invites you to The Atlantic Restaurant to enjoy a din­ing expe­ri­ence unpar­al­leled on Block Island. Relax and enjoy a roman­tic din­ner while sip­ping a glass of wine from our exten­sive and Award Win­ning wine list.

 

Restaurant

 

A vis­it to Block Island would not be com­plete with­out enjoy­ing a spec­tac­u­lar sun­set from our gra­cious veran­da or sprawl­ing lawn dot­ted with Adiron­dack chairs.



Cre­ate unfor­get­table mem­o­ries of your wed­ding or spe­cial event by hold­ing it at The Atlantic Inn. Enjoy the privacy and exclusivity of the entire Property and Grounds reserved for only you and your friends and family on for your special day. Our staff will attend to every detail to ensure that your occasion is a day you will remem­ber for­ev­er.

Our wedding team meets with every bride and groom to for­mu­late the perfect pro­pos­al for their spe­cial day. ­We provide our award winning food, majestic location, and the privacy of the whole property for the sole use of your party. Out team will ensure your special day is everything you dreamed it would be.

Start planning your wedding today.

author: Austin Crawford
title: Mohegan Bluffs - Mohegan Trail &  Payne Overlook
summary: The Mohegan Bluffs are an absolute must-see while visiting magical Block Island! The view from the 200-foot cliffs along Mohegan Trail, the island’s southernmost road, is beyond belief. Before descending down the 141 stairs to the beach below, stop and be amazed by the breathtaking view - sprawling deep blue ocean dotted with the country’s first offshore wind farm, and in the distance, you can see Montauk Point, on Long Island, NY. Take a picture at this iconic spot!
content:
The Mohegan Bluffs are an absolute must-see while visiting magical Block Island! The view from the 200-foot cliffs along Mohegan Trail, the island’s southernmost road, is beyond belief. Before descending down the 141 stairs to the beach below, stop and be amazed by the breathtaking view - sprawling deep blue ocean dotted with the country’s first offshore wind farm, and in the distance, you can see Montauk Point, on Long Island, NY. Take a picture at this iconic spot! 

On the shores below, enjoy a more private beach escape tucked below the remarkable cliffs. Lounge in the sun, take a nice barefoot stroll or bring along your surfboard to shred some waves. Keep in mind the only way down to the beach is by the main stairwell. We advise bringing your beach gear with you and packing lightly as the ascent back to the top is steep and breathtaking in a whole different way.

meta.title: Mohegan Bluffs

author: Robert Lucier
title: The Sacred Labyrinth
summary: Tucked away on the western side of Corn Neck Road is the Labyrinth. It’s a great stop on the way to another go-to destination - Sachem Pond. Halfway down the hill, look for discreet wooden steps over the stone wall.
content:
Tucked away on the western side of Corn Neck Road is the Labyrinth. It’s a great stop on the way to another go-to destination - Sachem Pond. Halfway down the hill, look for discreet wooden steps over the stone wall. 

At the top, you will find a quiet grassy overlook and a single path with many twists and turns made of stone. This is the Sacred Labyrinth. The Labyrinth is an ancient tool for prayer and mediation that has been carried out for thousands of years to inspire a feeling of transformation and wholeness.

Choose your approach - whether it be to follow the path to the center or begin from the center and walk outward - and delight in this true body and soul experience. There are chairs surrounding the Labyrinth and a box that has a journal in it available for all visitors to use and share their thoughts.

meta.title: The Sacred Labyrinth
meta.description: Walking the labyrinth is a universal ritual that has survived unchanged for thousands of years.

$10/$8  - Self-guided tour of the newly refurbished cottage in duplex. Learn about island history and art. See original 1880s furnishings in the light keepers’ quarters museum.

$20/$16 - Combo Tower tour—Guided only, NEW in 2021 visitors have access to outside watch deck with 360-degree view.

$15/$12 Tower Tour, Guided Only. Tower tours are limited in size. Once a group is formed, depart about every half hour. Take 56 steps up on a spiral cast-iron staircase. Allow time to enjoy your visit. Tour is about 20 minutes. Don’t forget your camera!

author: Austin Crawford
title: North Light & Cow Cove
summary: A popular historical site to visit on Block Island is the North Light. Inside the historical landmark is a small museum. The museum offers insight into the lighthouse's history and famous historic shipwrecks that occurred off the rocky shoreline of the Northern point of Block Island.
content:
A popular historical site to visit on Block Island is the North Light. Inside the historical landmark is a small museum. The museum offers insight into the lighthouse's history and famous historic shipwrecks that occurred off the rocky shoreline of the Northern point of Block Island. 

Block Island's National Wildlife Refuge surrounds the lighthouse. Be mindful of sensitive dune edges where beach grass grows and nesting areas of any type within the refuge. Enjoy the beautiful scenery and wildlife that surrounds the North Light! Cow cove is the small cove on the ocean side as you walk to the light from the parking lot.

The scenic Northern Light is located on the northernmost point of Block Island. Travel to the end of Corn Neck Road past Sachem Pond. At the end of Corn Neck Road, you will arrive at a parking lot where you must leave automobiles and bikes. Walk along the shore from the parking lot for around 10-15 minutes to arrive at the lighthouse. DO NOT SWIM at this beach due to STRONG RIPTIDES. 

The North Light building and exhibit is open to the public during the Summer season; check the website for current hours.

Webcam: https://blockislandnorthlight.com/

author: Cindy BlockIslandChamber
title: Block Island Nature Conservancy
summary: The Nature Conservancy and the Block Island Conservancy co-sponsor free, guided nature programs on Block Island throughout the year. Together, we offer many different ways to connect to the fields, marshes, and beaches that make the island so exceptional.
content:
The Nature Conservancy and the Block Island Conservancy co-sponsor free, guided nature programs on Block Island throughout the year. Together, we offer many different ways to connect to the fields, marshes, and beaches that make the island so exceptional.

A Last Great Place

In 1991, The Nature Conservancy named Block Island one of 12 “Last Great Places” in the western hemisphere. This special designation highlights the island’s abundance of rare plants and animals and the community’s unique support for conservation. The Conservancy set up a permanent field office on the island soon after, affirming our long-term commitment to protecting the nature of Block Island.

Today, nearly half the island is permanently protected for people and nature. It remains a wildlife hotspot, providing habitat for many plants and animals that disappeared from the rest of southern New England decades ago. The Conservancy’s Block Island Program maintains over 2,000 acres of habitat, offers hands-on discovery programs, and supports groundbreaking scientific research in the Great Salt Pond.

For all of this, we thank our supporters and partners -- many of whom have been with us since the very beginning -- for their generosity, trust, and vision for Block Island.



Provided By: Block Island Tourism Council

title: Blue Dory Inn
summary: The Blue Dory Inn sits at the head of the famous Crescent Beach and is only a short walking distance to the ferry, fine restaurants, and Island shops.
content:
The Blue Dory Inn sits at the head of the famous Crescent Beach and is only a short walking distance to the ferry, fine restaurants, and Island shops.

Our Block Island bed and breakfast will ensure your vacation to be stress-free and delightful. Join us in the eat-in kitchen for a delicious complimentary New England breakfast. Then take a towel and chair and follow the short path from our back door right down to Crescent beach, with its miles of unspoiled beauty. After a day at the beach, relax in the Blue Dory parlor where wine, hors d'oeuvres, and our famous Block Island Barnacle cookies are served every afternoon.

The Blue Dory has a lengthy history of a resident feline; our newest addition is the snuggly and infinitely entertaining little Simon. 


 

Book A Room Online

 



Eleven spacious guest rooms in our Block Island accommodations provide views of the ocean or the historic town. All the rooms have private baths, carpeting, and feature antique décor. A favorite spot of honeymooners, find out why The Boston Best Guide has voted The Blue Dory Inn "Block Island's Most Romantic Bed & Breakfast" since 1996. Book your stay at one of the premier Block Island inns by the beach.
